compiled_state.title: Polyglot Architect Foundation: multi-stack CI skeletons and pre-filled config for non-Python stacks
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
When a Ruby or Node/JS developer runs architect-foundation today, the generated dso-config.conf contains empty placeholders for all four commands.* keys because architect-foundation is not stack-aware. The developer must manually look up CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and fill in each key — a friction step that routinely produces misconfiguration. Additionally, CI skeleton generation uses static language-setup YAML with no hashFiles() conditionals. This epic makes architect-foundation pre-fill sensible defaults (derived from the same defaults table that Epics A and B establish) and generate CI skeletons with proper hashFiles() conditionals.

## Success Criteria
- architect-foundation pre-fills commands.test_runner, commands.lint, commands.format, and commands.format_check in the generated dso-config.conf for Python, Node/JS, and Ruby stacks; defaults derived from Epics A/B routing logic (not hard-coded separately); stacks without a defined default (Rust, unknown) write empty string with inline comment
- architect-foundation/SKILL.md and CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md updated with per-stack reference table enumerating pre-filled keys and default values for Python, Node/JS, Ruby, Rust
- CI skeleton generation uses hashFiles() conditional pattern: each language-setup step wrapped in appropriate lockfile check; all hashFiles() paths root-relative without leading slashes; per-stack conditional blocks are isolated so Epic F can append a Java block without modifying existing blocks
- Re-run preserves existing customized values; emits [DSO INFO] commands.<key> already set — skipping per preserved key
- INSTALL.md restructured into Required vs Optional-by-stack sections; this SC requires Epics A and B merged first and is deferrable without blocking epic closure if that sequencing is not met
- Validation: run architect-foundation on Python, Node/JS, and Ruby fixtures; assert all 4 commands.* keys present matching expected Epics A/B values; parse generated GitHub Actions YAML and assert hashFiles() conditionals with root-relative paths; re-run and assert preserved value

## Dependencies
- Epic A (87e9-0bc8): commands.test_runner key and defaults table must exist before Epic E pre-fills those values
- Epic B (3818-310d): commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check keys and per-stack defaults table must exist before Epic E derives and pre-fills those values; SC5 (INSTALL.md restructure) additionally requires both A and B merged

## Approach
Make architect-foundation stack-aware by reading the detected stack from detect-stack.sh output and pre-filling commands.* keys with the same defaults that onboarding writes. Update CI skeleton templates to use hashFiles() conditionals with isolated per-language blocks. Derive all default values from Epics A/B routing tables rather than duplicating them.

comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Epic complete: all 4 stories closed, all 6 SCs verified PASS, branch merged to main (v1.12.15)
